No Poverty.reason: The course is a 'Seminar on Development Assistance,' which is a core mechanism for Target 1.a (development cooperation) and 1.b (pro-poor strategies) to eradicate poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course covers humanitarianism and includes a specific reading on 'Famines in Historical Perspective,' directly addressing Target 2.1 (end hunger).

Good Health and Well-being.reason: A reading explicitly discusses 'Representing HIV/AIDS in Africa,' which directly relates to Target 3.3 (end epidemics of AIDS). The topic of humanitarianism is also relevant.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course covers 'Economic Development' and 'Aid for Trade,' directly aligning with Target 8.1 (economic growth) and Target 8.a (Aid for Trade support).
evidence: Aid for Trade: Is it Working?

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course on 'Development Assistance' is fundamentally about addressing global inequalities, directly supporting Target 10.b (encourage official development assistance).
evidence: Who Gives Foreign Aid and Why?

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course discusses 'Democracy Promotion,' 'conflict in Rwanda,' and 'Aiding Violence or Building Peace?', linking to Targets 16.1 (reduce violence) and 16.6 (strong institutions).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course 'Seminar on Development Assistance' is the embodiment of SDG 17, analyzing aid effectiveness, foreign aid, and North-South/South-South cooperation (Targets 17.2, 17.6, 17.9).
